item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1088)—Answers for Wednesday, June 3
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 3)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • BROWN
  • COLORFUL
  • JASMINE
  • GUMMY
  • STICKY
  • SUGARY
  • SUSHI
  • URSINE
  • EMPANADA
  • ARIE
  • FATAYER
  • BELL
  • PASTY
  • MOAN
  • SAMOSA
  • RAY

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Types of rice
  • Green – Words you’d use to describe candy shaped like bears
  • Blue – Delicious snack foods, either baked or fried
  • Purple – Disney princesses with a letter removed

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 3!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 3 (#1088)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BROWN, JASMINE, STICKY, SUSHI
  • Green – COLORFUL, GUMMY, SUGARY, URSINE
  • Blue – EMPANADA, FATAYER, PASTY, SAMOSA
  • Purple – ARIE, BELL, MOAN, RAY

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-june-3-1088/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Stuntman Hollywood Announced at State of Play
item.content:encoded:
A new Stuntman game has been announced, and Stuntman Hollywood has arrived with an adrenaline-filled trailer.

Stuntman Hollywood is an arcade racer that will put the players straight into a spectacular set piece and never let up. The upcoming title has been inspired by some of the greats in the genre, including the original Stuntman games, Burnout, and Split/Second.

Stuntman Series is Back

As the title is all about filming, players will get to replay iconic moments from Universal Pictures films and NBCUniversal TV shows like Fast & Furious, Back to the Future, Knight Rider, Miami Vice, and Death Race. You will perform all kinds of stunts across different shoots, using a variety of vehicles, including the DeLorean.

Stuntman Hollywood’s format will follow players as they shoot for different large-scale movies, with each movie split into episodes, each with its own vehicle, gameplay twists, environment, pace, objectives, and scenes. Each episode will also feature a different stunt-based challenge, such as performing drifts, holding a tight line, crashing through obstacles, dodging fire, riding on two wheels, etc.

These stunts are time-limited, and players have a limited number of takes, since movies don’t have unlimited budgets. But there’s also a free stunt mode, where the stuntman receives more freedom to be creative. The title will use a star system to reward stunts; the more stars, the better.

Other inclusions include a garage to showcase your utensils, tips, tiphies, and keepsakes, and it also displays your in-game progress. There are additional modes such as B-roll episodes, short filler films, and stunt arenas, which add more stunts, tougher challenges, and more unexpected scenes.

In other news, here’s everything announced during today’s State of Play event. What are your thoughts on Stuntman Hollywood being announced? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about upcoming PlayStation Plus Game Catalog and Classics Catalog games. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android
